label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is asking why smog has become a common concern. The options are A to D. In the passage, there's a part where it says, "As a popular online post said, air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone, despite your income or profession." Then it explains that people with higher incomes can pay for better food and water, but they can't escape the same air. This implies that smog affects everyone equally, regardless of their status or money. Option D says "Because nobody can avoid it." That matches the idea from the passage that the air we breathe is the same for everyone and can't be avoided. So, since smog is in the air and everyone has to breathe, no one can escape it. This leads to the concern being common. The other options don't fit as well. A says people have to pay higher prices, but the passage mentions that higher income people can pay for better water and food, not smog. B says a popular online post discussed it, which is true but not the main reason. The post is used to support the point
